---
title: "AHBA C3 Pseudobulk Developmental Trends (Multiple Datasets)"
format: md
jupyter: python3
---

## 1. Setup

### 1.1 Environment

```{python}
import os
import sys
import warnings
from IPython import get_ipython
_ip = get_ipython()
if _ip:
    _ip.run_line_magic('load_ext', 'autoreload')
    _ip.run_line_magic('autoreload', '2')
```

```{python}
def _find_repo_root(marker='.git'):
    current = os.path.abspath(os.getcwd())
    while True:
        if os.path.exists(os.path.join(current, marker)):
            return current
        parent = os.path.dirname(current)
        if parent == current:
            raise RuntimeError(f"Could not find '{marker}' above {os.getcwd()}")
        current = parent

_repo_root = _find_repo_root()
if os.path.join(_repo_root, 'code') not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(_repo_root, 'code'))

from environment import get_environment
_env = get_environment()
rds_dir  = _env['rds_dir']
code_dir = _env['code_dir']
ref_dir  = _env['ref_dir']

print(f"Environment : {_env['name']}")
print(f"  rds_dir  : {rds_dir}")
print(f"  code_dir : {code_dir}")
print(f"  ref_dir  : {ref_dir}")
```

### 1.2 Libraries

```{python}
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

if _env['name'] == 'local':
    os.environ['R_HOME'] = '/Users/richard/mambaforge/envs/scanpy/lib/R'

%load_ext rpy2.ipython
```

```{python}
%%R

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggpubr)
library(pwr)
```

```{python}
from hvg_investigation import (
    load_pseudobulk, setup_grn, load_cache,
    build_conditions_pearson_only,
    run_pseudobulk_pipeline,
)
```

## 2. Configuration

```{python}
# ── Dataset variants to compare ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# Each key is a short label; value is the pseudobulk_output/ directory.
DATASETS = {
    'VelWangPsychAD_100k':           rds_dir + '/Cam_snRNAseq/integrated/VelWangPsychAD_100k/pseudobulk_output',
    'VelWangPsychAD_100k_chemistry': rds_dir + '/Cam_snRNAseq/integrated/VelWangPsychAD_100k_chemistry/pseudobulk_output',
    'VelWangPsychAD_100k_dataset':   rds_dir + '/Cam_snRNAseq/integrated/VelWangPsychAD_100k_dataset/pseudobulk_output',
    'VelWangPsychAD_100k_pearson':   rds_dir + '/Cam_snRNAseq/integrated/VelWangPsychAD_100k_pearson/pseudobulk_output',
}

# ── Analysis parameters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
N_VALUES   = [2000, 8000]       # pearson_residuals n_top_genes to test
SCVI_LAYER = 'scanvi_normalized'

# Cache root: sub-folder per (integration variant, pseudobulk file type)
CACHE_ROOT = os.path.join(_repo_root, 'notebooks', 'ahbaC3_pseudobulk_datasets', '_cache')

# ── Age range (used for plots; best range also found via sensitivity grid) ────
CHILD_START = 1

print(f"Datasets : {list(DATASETS.keys())}")
print(f"N_VALUES : {N_VALUES}")
print(f"Cache    : {CACHE_ROOT}")
```

## 3. GRN Reference

```{python}
# Load the AHBA GRN once, using the chemistry pseudobulk as a gene-list
# reference (gene set is identical across all integration variants).
_ref_pb = os.path.join(
    list(DATASETS.values())[1], 'by_cell_class.h5ad')
_ref_adata = load_pseudobulk(_ref_pb, scvi_layer=SCVI_LAYER)
ahba_GRN, total_grn_genes = setup_grn(ref_dir, _ref_adata)
del _ref_adata
import gc; gc.collect()
print(f"GRN ready: {total_grn_genes} genes retained in pseudobulk adata")
```

## 4. Cell-Class Analysis

Pseudobulk aggregated per donor × cell_class (`by_cell_class.h5ad`).
Each sample represents one donor's mean expression profile for one cell class.

### 4.1 Load and Project

```{python}
cc_results = {}   # {integration_name: (scores_df, stats_df, final_df, hvg_df)}

for name, pb_dir in DATASETS.items():
    pb_file   = os.path.join(pb_dir, 'by_cell_class.h5ad')
    cache_dir = os.path.join(CACHE_ROOT, name, 'by_cell_class')
    print(f"\n{'='*60}\n{name}")

    cached = load_cache(cache_dir)
    if cached is not None:
        scores_df, stats_df, final_df, hvg_df = cached
    else:
        adata = load_pseudobulk(pb_file, scvi_layer=SCVI_LAYER)
        scores_df, stats_df, final_df, hvg_df = run_pseudobulk_pipeline(
            adata, ahba_GRN, total_grn_genes, N_VALUES, cache_dir)
        del adata; gc.collect()

    final_df['integration'] = name
    cc_results[name] = (scores_df, stats_df, final_df, hvg_df)
    print(f"  final_df rows: {len(final_df)}")

# Combine all datasets into one dataframe
cc_combined = pd.concat(
    [r[2] for r in cc_results.values()], ignore_index=True)
print(f"\nCombined cell-class df: {len(cc_combined)} rows, "
      f"{cc_combined['integration'].nunique()} integrations, "
      f"{cc_combined['cell_class'].nunique()} cell classes")
print(cc_combined.groupby(['integration', 'cell_class'])['individual']
      .nunique().unstack(fill_value=0).to_string())
```

### 4.2 Gene Overlap Summary

```{python}
for name, (_, stats_df, _, _) in cc_results.items():
    print(f"\n{name}")
    print(stats_df.to_string(index=False))
```

### 4.3 Sensitivity Analysis (cell-class level)

We run the gap-model sensitivity on the combined Excitatory pseudobulk scores
to find the best childhood / adolescence age range.

```{python}
%%R -i cc_combined -i code_dir -i N_VALUES

source(file.path(code_dir, 'hvg_plots.r'))
source(file.path(code_dir, 'sensitivity_gap_plots.r'))
source(file.path(code_dir, 'pseudobulk_dev_plots.r'))

# Prepare R data with pearson-only condition levels
df_cc <- prepare_pb_r_data(cc_combined, N_VALUES)

selected_conds <- c('pearson_2000', 'pearson_8000', 'all_genes')
CHILD_START    <- 1L
```

```{python}
%%R

# Run sensitivity on Excitatory pseudobulk (all integrations combined)
df_excit <- df_cc %>% filter(cell_class == 'Excitatory')

sens_cc <- compute_sensitivity_gap(
    df_excit, selected_conds,
    child_start = CHILD_START,
    child_ends  = c(7, 8, 9, 10),
    adol_starts = c(10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15),
    adol_ends   = c(19, 21, 23, 25))

best_cc     <- select_best_gap(sens_cc)
best_cc_ce  <- best_cc$child_end
best_cc_as  <- best_cc$adol_start
best_cc_ae  <- best_cc$adol_end

cat(sprintf(
    "Best age range (cell-class, all_genes baseline):\n  Childhood [%d, %d), Adolescence [%d, %d)  (p = %.4f, d = %.2f)\n",
    CHILD_START, best_cc_ce, best_cc_as, best_cc_ae,
    best_cc$p_value, best_cc$cohens_d))
```

```{python}
%%R -w 280 -h 220 -u mm -r 300

plot_gap_cohens_d(sens_cc)
```

```{python}
%%R -w 280 -h 220 -u mm -r 300

plot_gap_pvalue(sens_cc)
```

### 4.4 Developmental Trajectories by Cell Class

```{python}
%%R -w 300 -h 200 -u mm -r 300

plot_cellclass_trajectories(
    df_cc, 'pearson_2000',
    CHILD_START, best_cc_ce, best_cc_as, best_cc_ae,
    color_var = 'integration')
```

```{python}
%%R -w 300 -h 200 -u mm -r 300

plot_cellclass_trajectories(
    df_cc, 'pearson_8000',
    CHILD_START, best_cc_ce, best_cc_as, best_cc_ae,
    color_var = 'integration')
```

### 4.5 Box Plots by Age Range × Cell Class

```{python}
%%R -w 300 -h 200 -u mm -r 300

plot_cellclass_boxes(
    df_cc, 'pearson_2000',
    CHILD_START, best_cc_ce, best_cc_as, best_cc_ae,
    color_var = 'integration')
```

```{python}
%%R -w 300 -h 200 -u mm -r 300

plot_cellclass_boxes(
    df_cc, 'pearson_8000',
    CHILD_START, best_cc_ce, best_cc_as, best_cc_ae,
    color_var = 'integration')
```

### 4.6 Cross-Integration Sensitivity Comparison

```{python}
%%R -w 220 -h 140 -u mm -r 300

# Cohen's d for each integration × HVG condition at the best age range
sens_cc_by_int <- lapply(unique(df_cc$integration), function(intg) {
    df_sub <- df_cc %>% filter(cell_class == 'Excitatory', integration == intg)
    compute_sensitivity_gap(
        df_sub, selected_conds,
        child_start = CHILD_START,
        child_ends  = c(best_cc_ce),
        adol_starts = c(best_cc_as),
        adol_ends   = c(best_cc_ae)) %>%
    mutate(integration = intg)
})
sens_cc_by_int <- bind_rows(sens_cc_by_int)

sens_cc_by_int %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = cohens_d, fill = integration)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.7) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = 'Set1', name = 'Integration') +
    scale_x_discrete(name = 'HVG condition') +
    scale_y_continuous(name = "Cohen's d (Childhood vs Adolescence)") +
    theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
          legend.position = 'right') +
    ggtitle(
        "Cohen's d: Excitatory neurons, all integrations",
        subtitle = sprintf(
            "child < %dy, adol [%dy, %dy)",
            best_cc_ce, best_cc_as, best_cc_ae))
```

## 5. Cell-Type Analysis (L2-3 Focus)

Pseudobulk aggregated per donor × cell_type_aligned for Excitatory neurons
(`excitatory_by_celltype.h5ad`).  Each sample represents one donor's mean
expression profile for one specific excitatory cell type.

### 5.1 Load and Project

```{python}
ct_results = {}   # {integration_name: (scores_df, stats_df, final_df, hvg_df)}

for name, pb_dir in DATASETS.items():
    pb_file   = os.path.join(pb_dir, 'excitatory_by_celltype.h5ad')
    cache_dir = os.path.join(CACHE_ROOT, name, 'excitatory_by_celltype')
    print(f"\n{'='*60}\n{name}")

    cached = load_cache(cache_dir)
    if cached is not None:
        scores_df, stats_df, final_df, hvg_df = cached
    else:
        adata = load_pseudobulk(pb_file, scvi_layer=SCVI_LAYER)
        # cell_class column not in pseudobulk obs (filter was applied upstream);
        # add it so downstream R code can use it if needed.
        adata.obs['cell_class'] = 'Excitatory'
        scores_df, stats_df, final_df, hvg_df = run_pseudobulk_pipeline(
            adata, ahba_GRN, total_grn_genes, N_VALUES, cache_dir)
        del adata; gc.collect()

    final_df['integration'] = name
    ct_results[name] = (scores_df, stats_df, final_df, hvg_df)
    print(f"  final_df rows: {len(final_df)}")

# Combine all datasets
ct_combined = pd.concat(
    [r[2] for r in ct_results.values()], ignore_index=True)
print(f"\nCombined cell-type df: {len(ct_combined)} rows")
print(ct_combined.groupby(['integration', 'cell_type_aligned'])['individual']
      .nunique().unstack(fill_value=0).to_string())
```

### 5.2 Sensitivity Analysis (L2-3 neurons)

We restrict to L2-3 IT and related immature/newborn EN subtypes and run the
gap-model sensitivity to find the optimal age range.

```{python}
%%R -i ct_combined -i N_VALUES

df_ct <- prepare_pb_r_data(ct_combined, N_VALUES)

L23_TYPES <- c('EN-L2_3-IT', 'EN-Newborn', 'EN-IT-Immature', 'EN-Non-IT-Immature')

cat("Cell types present:\n")
print(sort(unique(df_ct$cell_type_aligned)))
```

```{python}
%%R

sens_L23 <- compute_L23_sensitivity(
    df_ct, selected_conds,
    L23_types   = L23_TYPES,
    child_start = CHILD_START,
    child_ends  = c(7, 8, 9, 10),
    adol_starts = c(10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15),
    adol_ends   = c(19, 21, 23, 25))

best_L23     <- select_best_gap(sens_L23)
best_L23_ce  <- best_L23$child_end
best_L23_as  <- best_L23$adol_start
best_L23_ae  <- best_L23$adol_end

cat(sprintf(
    "Best age range (L2-3 EN, all_genes baseline):\n  Childhood [%d, %d), Adolescence [%d, %d)  (p = %.4f, d = %.2f)\n",
    CHILD_START, best_L23_ce, best_L23_as, best_L23_ae,
    best_L23$p_value, best_L23$cohens_d))
```

```{python}
%%R -w 280 -h 220 -u mm -r 300

plot_gap_cohens_d(sens_L23)
```

```{python}
%%R -w 280 -h 220 -u mm -r 300

plot_gap_pvalue(sens_L23)
```

### 5.3 Developmental Trajectories: All Excitatory Cell Types

```{python}
%%R -w 320 -h 240 -u mm -r 300

plot_celltype_trajectories(
    df_ct, 'pearson_2000',
    CHILD_START, best_L23_ce, best_L23_as, best_L23_ae,
    color_var = 'integration')
```

```{python}
%%R -w 320 -h 240 -u mm -r 300

plot_celltype_trajectories(
    df_ct, 'pearson_8000',
    CHILD_START, best_L23_ce, best_L23_as, best_L23_ae,
    color_var = 'integration')
```

### 5.4 L2-3 Focus: Trajectory

```{python}
%%R -w 320 -h 200 -u mm -r 300

plot_L23_focus(
    df_ct,
    CHILD_START, best_L23_ce, best_L23_as, best_L23_ae,
    L23_types = L23_TYPES,
    color_var = 'cell_type_aligned')
```

### 5.5 L2-3 Focus: Cross-Integration Comparison

```{python}
%%R -w 320 -h 180 -u mm -r 300

plot_celltype_cross_integration(
    df_ct, 'EN-L2_3-IT', 'pearson_2000',
    CHILD_START, best_L23_ce, best_L23_as, best_L23_ae)
```

```{python}
%%R -w 320 -h 180 -u mm -r 300

plot_celltype_cross_integration(
    df_ct, 'EN-L2_3-IT', 'pearson_8000',
    CHILD_START, best_L23_ce, best_L23_as, best_L23_ae)
```

### 5.6 Cross-Integration Sensitivity: L2-3

```{python}
%%R -w 220 -h 140 -u mm -r 300

sens_L23_by_int <- lapply(unique(df_ct$integration), function(intg) {
    df_sub <- df_ct %>% filter(integration == intg,
                               cell_type_aligned %in% L23_TYPES)
    compute_sensitivity_gap(
        df_sub, selected_conds,
        child_start = CHILD_START,
        child_ends  = c(best_L23_ce),
        adol_starts = c(best_L23_as),
        adol_ends   = c(best_L23_ae)) %>%
    mutate(integration = intg)
})
sens_L23_by_int <- bind_rows(sens_L23_by_int)

sens_L23_by_int %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = cohens_d, fill = integration)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.7) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = 'Set1', name = 'Integration') +
    scale_x_discrete(name = 'HVG condition') +
    scale_y_continuous(name = "Cohen's d (Childhood vs Adolescence)") +
    theme_classic(base_size = 9) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
          legend.position = 'right') +
    ggtitle(
        "Cohen's d: L2-3 EN neurons, all integrations",
        subtitle = sprintf(
            "child < %dy, adol [%dy, %dy)",
            best_L23_ce, best_L23_as, best_L23_ae))
```

### 5.7 L2-3 Box Plots by Age Range

```{python}
%%R -w 300 -h 160 -u mm -r 300

df_L23_boxes <- df_ct %>%
    filter(C == 'C3+', cell_type_aligned %in% L23_TYPES) %>%
    group_by(condition, integration, individual, age_years, cell_type_aligned) %>%
    summarize(value = mean(value), .groups = 'drop') %>%
    mutate(age_range = case_when(
        age_years <  0             ~ 'Prenatal',
        age_years <  CHILD_START   ~ 'Infancy',
        age_years <  best_L23_ce   ~ 'Childhood',
        age_years <  best_L23_as   ~ 'Gap',
        age_years <  best_L23_ae   ~ 'Adolescence',
        TRUE                       ~ 'Adulthood'
    )) %>%
    mutate(age_range = factor(age_range, ordered = TRUE,
        levels = c('Prenatal', 'Infancy', 'Childhood',
                   'Gap', 'Adolescence', 'Adulthood')))

comparisons <- list(c('Childhood', 'Adolescence'),
                    c('Adolescence', 'Adulthood'))

df_L23_boxes %>%
    filter(condition %in% c('pearson_2000', 'pearson_8000')) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = age_range, y = value, fill = integration)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5,
                 position = position_dodge(0.8)) +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = integration),
                position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.15,
                                                dodge.width = 0.8),
                size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
    facet_grid(cell_type_aligned ~ condition, scales = 'free_y') +
    stat_compare_means(comparisons = comparisons,
                       label = 'p.signif', size = 3) +
    scale_x_discrete(name = NULL) +
    scale_y_continuous(name = 'C3+ Score (CPM)') +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = 'Set1', name = 'Integration') +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = 'Set1', guide = 'none') +
    theme_classic(base_size = 8) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7),
          strip.text = element_text(size = 7),
          legend.position = 'bottom') +
    ggtitle(sprintf(
        "C3+ by age range: L2-3 EN subtypes  (child < %dy, adol [%dy, %dy))",
        best_L23_ce, best_L23_as, best_L23_ae))
```

## 6. Summary Table

```{python}
%%R

# sens_cc_by_int and sens_L23_by_int each already contain only one age range
# (the respective best_cc / best_L23 parameters), so no further filter needed.
summary_tbl <- bind_rows(
    sens_cc_by_int %>%
        mutate(analysis = sprintf(
            'cell_class Excitatory  [child<%dy, adol[%dy,%dy))',
            best_cc_ce, best_cc_as, best_cc_ae)),
    sens_L23_by_int %>%
        mutate(analysis = sprintf(
            'cell_type L2-3 EN  [child<%dy, adol[%dy,%dy))',
            best_L23_ce, best_L23_as, best_L23_ae))
) %>%
    select(analysis, integration, condition, n_child, n_adol,
           cohens_d, p_value, p_star)

cat("Summary: Cohen's d and significance across integrations and HVG methods\n\n")
print(summary_tbl, n = 100)
```
